critique.summary: The article is a comprehensive gallery of large language model (LLM) architectures, detailing various models' configurations, parameter scales, attention mechanisms, and key design features. It compiles information from two main source articles and provides links to technical reports and configuration files for each model.
mainPoints: The gallery covers a wide range of LLMs including dense, sparse MoE, hybrid, and linear-attention models with parameter counts ranging from a few billion to over a trillion.
Each model entry includes details such as decoder type, attention mechanism, scale, date of release, and notable architectural innovations or design choices.
The article references two main source articles that provide context and explanations for the architectural choices and evolution of these models.
The gallery is regularly updated with new models and invites readers to report inaccuracies or issues.
critique.truthfulness: The article appears to be a factual and detailed compilation of LLM architectures, referencing specific technical reports and configuration files for each model. The information aligns with known trends in LLM development such as the use of mixture-of-experts (MoE), gated attention, and various normalization techniques. The dates and model details correspond plausibly to the timeline of LLM advancements. However, the article itself is a secondary compilation and does not independently verify the claims beyond citing source articles and technical reports. The source articles mentioned are specialized technical write-ups by a known AI researcher, which lends credibility but does not substitute for independent journalistic verification.
considerations: The article does not provide performance benchmarks or evaluations of the models, focusing solely on architectural details.
It lacks multiple perspectives or critical analysis of the models' practical impact or limitations.
The source articles are from a single author and may reflect a particular viewpoint or selection bias in model coverage.
The article assumes a high level of technical knowledge, which may limit accessibility for general audiences.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion centers around appreciation for a visualization of LLM architectures, with users expressing admiration and suggesting enhancements like evolutionary sorting and scaled views. Some users share related resources and inquire about insights gained during the creation process.

comments.author: iroddis
comments.text:
This is amazing, such a nice presentation. It reminds me of the Neural Network Zoo [1], which was also a nice visualization of different architectures.

[1] https://www.asimovinstitute.org/neural-network-zoo/
comments.analysis: The comment provides a relevant comparison to a known visualization, adding context and value to the discussion.

comments.author: wood_spirit
comments.text:
Lovely!

Is there a sort order? Would be so nice to understand the threads of evolutions and revolution in the progression. A bit of a family tree and influence layout? It would also be nice to have a scaled view so you can sense the difference in sizes over time.
comments.analysis: This comment offers constructive feedback and suggestions for improving the visualization's clarity and usefulness.

comments.author: gasi
comments.text: So cool — thanks for sharing! Here’s a zoomable version of the diagram: https://zoomhub.net/LKrpB
comments.analysis: The comment shares a helpful resource that complements the original visualization, enhancing user experience.

comments.author: mvrckhckr
comments.text: What a great idea and nice execution.
comments.analysis: A brief positive reaction that adds little analytical depth but reflects genuine appreciation.

comments.author: FailMore
comments.text: Thanks! This is cool. Can you tell me if you learnt anything interesting/surprising when pulling this together? As in did it teach you something about LLM Architecture that you didn't know before you began?
comments.analysis: The comment poses a thoughtful question but does not provide information or analysis itself.
